name: Dominguez Family Cactus Stew
recipeIngredient: Toasted cumin seed, crushed or ground (1 t to 2 ts)
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the cactus paddle by holding it between the nodes of the prickly spines. Slice off the nodes with a sharp paring knife. Keep a damp paper towel nearby to wipe your knife and prevent the spines from sticking.
2. Trim around the edge of the paddle where the spines are closer together. Do not peel the cactus, only remove the nodes and spines on both sides. Trim off the blunt end where it was cut from the plant.
3. Cut the paddle into 1/4-inch strips about 2 inches long.
4. Bring 4 quarts of water with 1 tablespoon salt to a boil in a large pot. Add onion, garlic, cilantro, and tomatillo husks. Drop in the cactus strips. Blanch the cactus until just crisp tender, about 8 to 10 minutes.
5. Drain the cactus strips in a colander and rinse with cool water.
6. Place the meat in a pot with 3 cups of water. Simmer, covered, until the water is almost gone. Tilt the pot and drain off the fat.
7. Add garlic, red chile sauce, and spices to the drained meat. Simmer about 30 minutes. Taste for salt, then stir in the drained nopalitos. Simmer for an additional 20 minutes.
8. Blend masa harina and water to make a paste. Whisk it into the stew and simmer for 5 more minutes. Stir in cilantro. If desired, add toasted japones chile for spiciness.
9. Serve hot with warm tortillas.
